intro: The clearest split is that Dyte uses a freemium model and Stream uses a subscription model. Dyte is Video call and audio room SDK with custom UI components, recording, and live streaming; Stream is Activity feed and chat API infrastructure with scalable feed aggregation, reactions, and moderation. The data points Dyte at chat applications and live collaboration features, and Stream at chat applications and live collaboration features. Pricing: Dyte is freemium from $0.00; Stream is subscription from $399.00/mo. Lock-in is medium for Dyte and medium for Stream, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 5/5 vs 4/5. On listed strengths, Dyte emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features, and Stream emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features. Only Stream lists an enterprise tier in the data. Honest trade-off: neither fits static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Dyte is for chat applications; Stream is for chat applications; decide based on which pricing model fits your budget.
verdict_a: Choose Dyte if a freemium pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.
verdict_b: Choose Stream if a subscription pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.

faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $0.00 for Dyte and $399.00/mo for Stream. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Dyte to Stream?
faq.a: Dyte is tagged with medium lock-in (sdk tied but data portable). Stream is tagged with medium lock-in (feed/chat api, data exportable). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Dyte or Stream?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Stream a good alternative to Dyte?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches chat applications, since both list the same fit. Stream is flagged as not for static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
